history.subject: Prove geometric generic flatness (Nitsure §4): over an integral locally noetherian base S, a finitely presented module sheaf on a finite-type X → S has flat sections over all affine pairs below a non-empty V = D(f). New file Picard/GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean: the mixed-base stability lemma Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra (flatness over the base survives localizing at a fibre-algebra submonoid — axiom-clean, closes a Mathlib gap), the chart-basic core (chart freeness at g localizes to any basic open above D(g), via the qcqs section-localization engine and base-ring descent), and the two-layer basic-open reduction via Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span plus two flat_iff_of_isLocalization exchanges. genericFlatness's own proof is complete and kernel-checked; it is conditional (sorryAx) only through genericFlatnessAlgebraic, whose r3 induction proof (session 0027, ended at rate limit, integrated unverified) fails the batch kernel build at three localized-module instance walls and is honestly re-opened as a documented typed sorry (r3 text preserved in ledger 6c30537; its sub-lemma layer survives kernel-checked). FlatteningStratification.lean stays Mathlib-only-imports so its heavy dévissage proofs keep their instance environment.

Blueprint: geometric-glue chapter for generic flatness and the affine finite-sections cluster. Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex gains six glue nodes (mixed-base flatness stability, two appLE restriction compatibilities, section-flatness transport, chart-basic core, two-layer basic-open reduction — all \leanok with complete proofs) and thm:generic_flatness gets its real proof (chart supply + per-chart generic freeness + product denominator + the reduction), statement-\leanok only since its cone runs through the re-opened algebraic core; also fixes the malformed theorem header. Picard_QuotScheme.tex gains the Stacks 01PC finite-type cluster (finite generation from a finite generating family of a tilde via the tilde–Γ adjunction; the chart form over a presenting cover member; the affine-neighbourhood chart supply), all \leanok.
Blueprint: fix garbled counit-factorization clause in module_finite_of_tilde_genSections proof (Stacks 01PC node); the adjunction transpose satisfies $\widetilde t \mathbin{;} \varepsilon = \pi'$

T12 generic freeness domain core CLOSED: the Noether-normalisation strong induction (genericallyFree_quotient_prime_of_fibre_dim_le) kernel-checks — the r3 localized-module walls are defused by extracting the generic-rank exact sequence 0 → D^r → M → T → 0 (T killed by one d ≠ 0) into the clean-context lemma exists_generic_rank_comparison; genericFlatnessAlgebraic (Stacks 051R) and the geometric genericFlatness (Nitsure §4) are now axiom-clean [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]. Blueprint: new node lem:generic_rank_comparison, proof-\leanok flips for the domain core and both generic-flatness theorems.

T12 statement repair: the finite-strata flattening statements (flatLocusReduction, flatLocusAssembly, flatteningStratification, _universal, .ofCurve) require a NOETHERIAN base, not merely locally noetherian — with only local noetherianity the finite family is impossible (on ⊔ₙ 𝔸ⁿ with the nested-flag sheaf ⊕_{k≤n} O_{V_k}, component n forces ≥ n+1 strata). Lean hypotheses strengthened to IsNoetherian, blueprint bodies aligned with Nitsure's wording, counterexample recorded in docstring + blueprint remark. The ℕ-indexed n=0 case stays locally noetherian (fibre rank is the global index).
Flatness under pushout base change (Stacks 00HI module form): B⊗[A]M is S⊗[R]M for a ring pushout, via of_lift_unique; Module.Flat.of_isPushout

Affine pieces of fibre-product squares: affinity, section-ring pushout (via Mathlib pushoutSection), per-piece flatness/finiteness transfer via Lane F section formula; de-privatize pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap{,_sectionLinearEquiv}
genericFlatness refactor: quasi-coherent core genericFlatness_of_finite_sections (chart supply as hypothesis), 01PC wrapper unchanged statement
Flatness over the base is affine-local (Stacks 00HT style): chart flatness on an affine cover implies all affine pairs, via two-layer basic-open reduction
Reduced vanishing-ideal subschemes (radical ideal sheaf, reduced affine pieces, integral when irreducible) + CoherentSheafFlat transport along sheaf isos
